sections.title: Performance Analysis: Axis CLI
sections.content: **Note:** This is a **Go CLI application** (not React/web). There are no React components, no N+1 database queries, and no browser rendering concerns.

sections.title: 1. N+1 Query Patterns
sections.content:
**Not applicable.** This codebase uses an in-memory `MemoryStateStore` with Go maps. There's no ORM or database layer.

---

sections.title: 2. Unnecessary Re-renders in React
sections.content:
**Not applicable.** No React/UI code exists in this codebase.

---

sections.title: 3.1 `tool.Registry.List()` - No Schema Caching
sections.content:
**File:** `internal/model/tool/tool.go:62-70`

```go
func (r *Registry) List() []types.ToolDefinition {
    r.mu.RLock()
    defer r.mu.RUnlock()
    defs := make([]types.ToolDefinition, 0, len(r.tools))
    for _, t := range r.tools {
        defs = append(defs, t.Schema())  // Copies every call
    }
    return defs
}
```

**Issue:** `Schema()` is called on every invocation. If tool schemas are static, cache the result.

**Fix:**
```go
type Registry struct {
    mu       sync.RWMutex
    tools    map[string]Tool
    scopes   map[string][]string
    schemas  []types.ToolDefinition // cached
}
```

sections.title: 3.2 `ContractExecutor` Field Validation - Linear Search
sections.content:
**File:** `internal/contract/executor/executor.go:216-235`

```go
for _, field := range contract.InputSchema.Fields {  // O(n) per field
    // ...
}
```

**Issue:** For contracts with many fields, this is O(n) per validation. Repeated contract validations do the same work.

**Fix:** Consider caching validated contracts or pre-indexing fields by name:
```go
type AgentContract struct {
    InputSchema  *Schema
    inputFields  map[string]FieldDef  // index on first use
}
```

---

sections.title: 4.1 Busy-Wait Polling in `runTaskLoop`
4.2 Unbounded History in `executeMultiTurn`
sections.content:
**File:** `internal/contract/executor/executor.go:139`

```go
var history []types.ModelMessage  // Grows unbounded
```

**Issue:** In long tool-calling chains, history accumulates indefinitely within a single execution.

**Fix:** Add a cap:
```go
const maxHistorySize = 100
if len(history) >= maxHistorySize {
    history = history[len(history)-maxHistorySize:]
}
```

sections.title: 4.3 New Map Allocation Per Call in `detectCircularDependencies`
sections.content:
**File:** `internal/kernel/scheduler/scheduler.go:59`

```go
if err := s.detectCircularDependencies(task.TaskID, task.Dependencies, make(map[string]bool)); err != nil {
```

**Issue:** Creates a new `visited` map on every call, causing GC pressure.

**Fix:** Use recursion with a `visited` set passed by reference, or use an `int` timestamp pattern:
```go
type SchedulerImpl struct {
    // ... existing fields
    visitClock uint64
    visited    map[string]uint64 // taskID -> visitID
}
```

---

sections.title: 5.1 `parseSLA` Called Every Retry
sections.content:
**File:** `internal/kernel/orchestrator/orchestrator.go:170`

```go
func (o *Orchestrator) executeTask(ctx context.Context, task *types.AgentTask) {
    timeoutMs, maxRetries, failureClass, backoff := parseSLA(task.Metadata)
    // ...
    for attempt := 0; attempt <= maxRetries; attempt++ {  // Same values
```

**Issue:** `parseSLA` parses metadata once but values are constant for the task lifetime.

**Fix:** Parse once before the retry loop (already done), but extract to a local to make intent clearer:
```go
timeoutMs, maxRetries, failureClass, backoff := parseSLA(task.Metadata)
for attempt := 0; attempt <= maxRetries; attempt++ {
    // use captured values
```

sections.title: 5.2 Linear Enum Lookups
5.3 `time.Now()` Called Twice Per Status Update
sections.content:
**File:** `internal/kernel/scheduler/scheduler.go:304-317`

```go
task.Status = status
now := time.Now()

switch status {
case types.TaskStatusRunning:
    task.StartedAt = &now
case types.TaskStatusCompleted, types.TaskStatusFailed:
    task.CompletedAt = &now
}

state := types.TaskState{
    Task:      task,
    UpdatedAt: time.Now(),  // Second call
}
```

**Issue:** Two `time.Now()` calls per status update.

**Fix:** Use single timestamp:
```go
now := time.Now()
task.Status = status
switch status {
case types.TaskStatusRunning:
    task.StartedAt = &now
case types.TaskStatusCompleted, types.TaskStatusFailed:
    task.CompletedAt = &now
}
state := types.TaskState{Task: task, UpdatedAt: now}
```

---

sections.title: Summary Table
sections.content:
| Category | Issue | Severity | Location |
|----------|-------|----------|----------|
| Cache | `Registry.List()` recreates schemas | Medium | `tool.go:62` |
| Cache | Field linear search in validation | Low | `executor.go:216` |
| Memory | Busy-wait 100ms polling | Medium | `orchestrator.go:134` |
| Memory | Unbounded history growth | Low | `executor.go:139` |
| Memory | Map allocation per cycle check | Low | `scheduler.go:59` |
| Compute | Redundant `time.Now()` calls | Low | `scheduler.go:304` |
| Compute | Linear enum lookups | Low | `executor.go:352` |

codeBlocks.code: if err := s.detectCircularDependencies(task.TaskID, task.Dependencies, make(map[string]bool)); err != nil {

type FieldDef struct {
    Name     string
    Type     FieldType
    Required bool
    Enum     []string
    enumSet  map[string]bool  // computed once
}

rawOutputPreview:
## Performance Analysis: Axis CLI

**Note:** This is a **Go CLI application** (not React/web). There are no React components, no N+1 database queries, and no browser rendering concerns.

### 1. N+1 Query Patterns
**Not applicable.** This codebase uses an in-memory `MemoryStateStore` with Go maps. There's no ORM or database layer.

---

### 2. Unnecessary Re-renders in React
**Not applicable.** No React/UI code exists in this codebase.

---

### 3. Caching Opportunities

#### 3.1 `tool.Registry.List()` - No Schema Caching
**File:** `internal/model/tool/tool.go:62-70`

```go
func (r *Registry) List() []types.ToolDefinition {
    r.mu.RLock()
    defer r.mu.RUnlock()
    defs := make([]types.ToolDefinition, 0, len(r.tools))
    for _, t := range r.tools {
        defs = append(defs, t.Schema())  // Copies every call
    }
    return defs
}
```

**Issue:** `Schema()` is called on every invocation. If tool schemas are static, cache the result.

**Fix:**
```go
type Registry struct {
    mu       sync.RWMutex
    tools    map[string]Tool
    scopes   map[string][]string
    schemas  []types.ToolDefinition // cached
}
```

#### 3.2 `ContractExecutor` Field Validation - Linear Search
**File:** `internal/contract/executor/executor.go:216-235`

```go
for _, field := range contract.InputSchema.Fields {  // O(n) per field
    // ...
}
```

**Issue:** For contracts with many fields, this is O(n) per validation. Repeated contract validations do the same work.

**Fix:** Consider caching validated contracts or pre-indexing fields by name:
```go
type AgentContract struct {
    InputSchema  *Schema
    inputFields  map[string]FieldDef  // index on first use
}
```

---

### 4. Memory Leaks / Waste

#### 4.1 Busy-Wait Polling in `runTaskLoop`
**File:** `internal/kernel/orchestrator/orchestrator.go:129-154`

```go
for {
    // ...
    available := o.workerLimit - len(o.workerSem)
    if available <= 0 {
        select {
        case <-ctx.Done():
            return
        case <-time.Af
